calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, For Leave To Defend, And For Reinstatement To Possession
Off Calendar Aug-05-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THOMAS S. MILLER, III'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, For Leave To Defend, And For Reinstatement To Possession:
APPEARANCE: Charles T. Ramsey, Esq. Of Law Offices of Charles T. Ramsey 510/986-0455 for pltf & opposing party; Jessica L. Chylik, Esq. Of The Epstein Group 863-5718 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Court conducted an evidentiary hearing. Pltf's witnesses: Christy Ruiz & Al Simms, both sworn & examined. Deft's witnesses: Thomas S. Miller, III & Rosalie Honor, both sworn & examined. The following exhibits were identified & admitted into evidence: Pltf's exhibit 1 - letter dated 5/4/04 addressed to Thomas Miller from Christy Ruiz; pltf's exhibit 2 - sealed envelope addressed to Thomas Miller from Law Offices of Charles Tillman Ramsey; pltf's exhibit 3 - U.S. Postal Service certified mail receipt. Argued and the Court took the matter under submission. Court requested that counsel submit simultaneous supplemental briefs by noon on 07/30/04. Exhibits returned to pltf's counsel. (301/JLW/DS) Court Reporter Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
